title: Coming soon
summary: This introductory post announces "Engineering the Future," a series dedicated to exploring truth and understanding.

title: Going to California
summary: The author reflects on a trip to San Francisco, realizing his unsuitability for private equity and his desire to work on the fringes of change.

title: The Cemetery
summary: Reflecting on a visit to a graveyard, the author questions the purpose of gravestones as status symbols and reminders of mortality.

title: Nephra - 04/21/2022
summary: An update to a team about the Nephra startup, discussing market opportunity, funding challenges, and the need for product development and grants.

title: A New Religion
summary: The author discusses religion with a friend, concluding that religions serve as societal rulebooks and questioning the existence of God.

title: Being Alone
summary: The author explores the enjoyment of solitude, contrasting it with societal notions of fun and happiness, and linking it to purpose-seeking.

title: A Year of Startups
summary: This extensive essay details lessons learned over a year of founding a quantitative investment manager and a medical device startup, emphasizing entrepreneurship as a learned mindset.

title: Business == Execution
summary: The author argues that business is fundamentally about execution, criticizing traditional business education and highlighting the importance of doing.

title: THE LOGOS
summary: The author explores the concept of "The Logos" as an underlying truth of the Universe, advocating for a return to philosophical thinking and scientific progress.

title: HAVE CONVICTIONS
summary: Drawing inspiration from Steve Jobs, the author encourages readers to challenge existing structures, define their own lives, and pursue their unique interests.

title: Biotech Week in Review | 02-17-2023
summary: This review discusses trends in biotech startup funding, the potential for more founder-led ventures, and the challenges posed by FDA regulations and Eroom’s law.

title: What is life?
summary: The author begins a series on life's purpose, urging readers to question meaning and values from first principles to avoid regret.

title: Which direction to live it.
summary: Continuing the exploration of life's purpose, this essay delves deeper into fundamental questions about human existence and the path one should take.

title: Biotech Week in Review | 03-03-2023
summary: This review covers AI applications in medical diagnosis (e.g., hypertrophic cardiomyopathy) and discusses the challenges of non-invasive glucose monitoring.

title: Biotech Update - 04.21.2023
summary: The author discusses contagious cancers in Tasmanian devils, tumor evolution, and the potential for therapeutic development based on such phenomena.

title: AMBITIONS (UNEDITED)
summary: The author uses Nietzsche's metamorphoses (camel, lion, child) to illustrate personal growth through overcoming, freedom, and creation.

title: ESSAY 8: AMBITIONS (EDITED)
summary: An edited version of the previous essay, it discusses Nietzsche's metamorphoses as stages of personal growth and the challenge of finding purpose in adulthood.

title: Biotech Week in Review - 05-12-2023
summary: This review highlights DNA storage as a potential data solution and the significance of mapping the human pangenome for understanding genetic diversity.

title: ESSAY 9: VALUE, SOCIETY, MONEY & WORK
summary: The author explores the concept of work as a fundamental human drive for survival and thriving, linking it to societal innovation and investment.

title: ESSAY 10: ABSTRACTION VS FIRST PRINCIPLES
summary: This essay contrasts abstraction (pattern recognition) with first principles thinking (questioning fundamental assumptions) as necessary modes for human thought.

title: ESSAY 11: TIME
summary: The author reflects on time as the most crucial universal constraint, emphasizing its importance in planning and maximizing personal output.

title: △ - Everything I Know Now
summary: A comprehensive reflection on life experiences, advocating for focusing on immediate growth ("cultivate our garden") rather than solely abstract problems.

title: Culture
summary: The author traces his evolving understanding of culture from superficial rituals to a company's underlying behaviors, principles, and values.

title: Project Delta (∆)
summary: The author proposes "Project Delta" to use long-form writing and LLMs to extract an individual's "ideological fingerprint" for self-understanding.

title: Information Theory of Startups
summary: Applying Claude Shannon's information theory, the author argues that "unlikely information" and doing "unlikely things" hold significant value in entrepreneurship.

title: Entrepreneurial Risk
summary: The author challenges conventional risk models in entrepreneurship, advocating for trusting human instincts and navigating complexity with limited data.

title: Deep Learning's Future
summary: This essay provides an overview of deep learning's foundational principles, framing the future through derived principles and philosophical takes on AI's impact.

title: Project Delta ∆
summary: A refined version of "Project Delta," emphasizing the use of AI to help individuals understand their identity and life trajectory.

title: Return
summary: The author reflects on the "things you return to" in life, linking this concept to self-understanding and the inspiration behind Project Delta.

title: Launching Irreverent Capital
summary: The author announces "Irreverent Capital," a fund dedicated to building important businesses and fostering innovation by challenging existing wisdom.

title: General Personal Embeddings
summary: The author argues for a universal, neutral infrastructure for personal embeddings, enabling machines to understand individuals deeply while protecting data ownership.

title: “Google, Tear Down This Wall”
summary: The author critiques the current state of personalization driven by Big Tech, advocating for user-owned digital identities and a new era of AI-driven understanding.

title: Deep User Understanding from Day One
summary: This post describes a product vision for instant, deep user understanding via AI, enabling personalized experiences from the moment a user signs up.

title: Structure, People, and Chaos
summary: The author explores the human struggle against entropy, advocating for embracing chaos and irreverence to foster creativity within an over-structured world.

title: Politzki’s Law
summary: The author proposes "Politzki’s Law," stating that humans maintain an advantage over computers in navigating indeterminate, ambiguous, and complex problems with sparse data.

title: Reflections on a Quarter Century of Life
summary: A comprehensive reflection on 25 years of life, emphasizing the difficulty and importance of self-understanding and navigating the world with purpose.

title: Vacations
summary: The author re-evaluates vacations, finding their value in providing a necessary reset from the pressures of work, whether over-structured or overly abstract.

title: Jean - Software for the Individual
summary: The author introduces "Jean," a portfolio company focused on personal software that uses LLMs to map individual belief systems and values, aligning with a vision of individualism.

title: The Irreverent Act
summary: This extensive essay frames starting a new company as a creative act and a test of identity, emphasizing the cultural biases and irreverence needed for technological progress.

title: A → B
summary: The author discusses business as the process of moving from A to B, stressing the importance of strategic thinking and knowing "what," "why," and "how" before execution.

title: MCP, A2A, and the Future of AI Infra
summary: The author discusses the evolving landscape of AI infrastructure, including MCP and A2A, and how it informs the development of Jean as a "universal gas station for user understanding."

title: B2C Memory Layer
summary: The author outlines a vision for a B2C memory layer that protects individual data rights, iterating on solutions based on market learnings for Jean.

title: Automating the World
summary: The author posits that most work will be automated, drawing parallels with biotech's slowness to adopt AI, and emphasizing the human bottleneck to technological distribution.

title: Self-Healing Software
summary: The author discusses the advent of self-healing software, inspired by Claude Shannon's error-correcting codes, as a way to automate bug reproduction and free up human focus.

title: Jean Memory: The Necessity of Irreverence
summary: This essay, purportedly written by Jean Memory itself, introduces the product and argues for the necessity of irreverence to overcome societal stagnation and foster genuine creation.

title: Building Jean Memory
summary: The author details the soft launch and initial success of Jean Memory, reiterating its vision to build infrastructure for computers to understand humans deeply.

title: Engineering Context
summary: The author explores the theoretical and practical aspects of "AI Memory" and "context engineering," aiming to provide relevant personal context for AI inference.

title: The Jean Manifesto
summary: This manifesto asserts that computers now understand humans, advocating for AI infrastructure that provides personal context to applications while protecting individual data.

title: Boldness
summary: The author reflects on the power of simple action and boldness, drawing on Francis Bacon's observations and linking it to the entrepreneurial spirit and the "perfect pivot."

title: Outsiders
summary: The author re-examines Mikhail Tal's chess genius, linking his "unlikely moves" to a unique outsider's understanding of the game and the freedom to challenge the status quo.

title: AI Memory: Pre-History
summary: This is the first part of a comprehensive report, providing a pre-history of AI Memory and asserting that good context is more valuable than a good model.

title: AI Memory: Technical Foundations
summary: The second part of the AI Memory report, it delves into advanced memory architectures, design principles, and argues that RAG is a transitional technology.

title: AI Memory: Market Dynamics
summary: The third part of the AI Memory report, it reviews current use cases (B2C, B2B2C, B2B) and market providers, analyzing where value will accrue.

title: AI Memory: Future Outlook
summary: The final part of the AI Memory report synthesizes previous sections into four forward-looking theses, predicting the evolution from RAG to agentic memory and the flow of value.

title: Self-Determination
summary: The author explores "Innerlichkeit" (inwardness) through Nietzsche and Freud, discussing how natural instincts are sublimated inwards when constrained by external forces.

title: The State of AI Memory Infrastructure: 2026 Report
summary: This executive summary of the "State of AI Memory Infrastructure: 2026 Report" highlights the "Context Crisis" in LLMs and proposes a unified standard for user understanding.
